Oracle Events
OE 1 - Search emails using queries like "BambooHR," "native connector," or "onboarding" to find Julian's outbound email (email-onboarding-003) where he told Rachel that MoveOps has a native BambooHR connector and engineering would have API credentials ready within 24 hours of starting. Tool: search_emails.
OE 2 -Search emails using queries like "BambooHR," "CSV," or "bulk upload" to find Rachel's inbound reply (email-onboarding-004) where she confirms her IT team is working on exporting a CSV from BambooHR — indicating she used a manual CSV export rather than the promised native API integration. Tool: search_emails.
OE 3 - Retrieve the full conversation history of channel C009 (#root-cause-aws-spike) to find Samira's root cause analysis, Lena's engineering breakdown, Marcus's cost concerns, Elena's directive, and the confirmation that there was zero client-facing impact. Catalina explicitly confirmed in this channel that Rachel was happy with the portal and had no complaints. Tool: conversations_history on channel C009.
OE 4 - Search emails using queries like "root cause," "AWS," or "Vantage Distributed" to find Samira's formal RCA email (email-rootcause-001) sent to Marcus and Elena, and Elena's directive reply (email-rootcause-002) confirming no blame for Julian, endorsing the deal, and mandating an infrastructure cost estimator tool before the next enterprise pitch. Tool: search_emails.
OE 5 - Search Slack using queries like "BambooHR," "API credentials," or "admin accounts" to find Catalina's message in C002 (#customer-engagement) confirming that all three admin accounts were set up and BambooHR API credentials were generated and shared securely before onboarding. Tool: conversations_search_messages.
OE 6 - Look up the Terraform Digital account in the CRM (also tracked internally as Vantage Distributed) using a query like "Terraform" or "Vantage Distributed" to find the account record, current AM (Mina Hashimoto), account status (Onboarding), and the Reply-All incident engagement log. Company ID: company-terraform-digital. Tool: crm_search_companies or crm_get_company.
OE 7 - Look up Rachel Nguyen's contact information using a CRM contact lookup to retrieve her verified email address rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com before sending the follow-up email. Her contact record exists in the CRM under company company-terraform-digital. Tool: crm_search_contacts or crm_get_contact with query "Rachel Nguyen" or "Terraform."
OE 8 - Send an email from julian.brooks@moveops.com to rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com, CC mina.hashimoto@moveops.com. Content should cover: a friendly check-in on the onboarding experience, a specific question about whether the BambooHR API integration is working or if her team is still using CSV exports, and an offer to get engineering engaged if the native connector was never fully delivered. Tool: send_email.
OE 9 - Post a message in channel C007 (#customer-support) summarizing the audit findings: the AWS spike caused zero client-facing impact and Rachel was never informed about it (correct), the BambooHR native connector status is unverified and needs engineering follow-up, and Mina Hashimoto is AM of record for all future Terraform account coordination. Tool: conversations_add_message with channel C007.
OE 10 - Create a CRM engagement on the Terraform Digital account (company-terraform-digital) documenting the post-delivery audit: what was investigated, key finding that BambooHR integration is unverified, and that Julian reached out to Rachel directly. Tool: crm_create_engagement.

1 An email was sent (via send_email) to [rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com](mailto:rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com) with [mina.hashimoto@moveops.com](mailto:mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) in the CC field.
outcome

2 The email body sent to [rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com](mailto:rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com) (visible in send_email parameters) references the BambooHR integration or asks whether Rachel's team is using the API connector or CSV exports (e.g., "BambooHR," "API integration," "native connector," "CSV," or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✗✓ 83%

✓Run #3The email body extensively references BambooHR, the native API connector/integration, and CSV exports. It directly asks Rachel whether she is still managing employee data via CSV or if the BambooHR sync was set up.
✓Run #4The email body explicitly references BambooHR, the native API integration promise, CSV exports, and asks Rachel whether her team is syncing via CSV exports or whether the API integration is fully set up. The BambooHR topic is a central focus of the email.
✗Run #5No email was sent to rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com at all. The criterion requires the email body sent to Rachel to reference BambooHR. Since no email to Rachel exists, this criterion fails.
✓Run #6The email body explicitly references BambooHR, the native API integration, the 24-hour promise, CSV exports, and asks Rachel how the BambooHR side is currently working. All required elements are present.

outcome

No client-facing issue.' and 'The AWS spike was an internal cost/pricing issue, not a service failure.' This clearly references zero client-facing impact.
✓Run #4The Slack message in #customer-support explicitly states: 'There was **no client-facing impact** — no downtime, no errors, no degraded experience on Rachel's end. The issue was purely an internal pricing/margin gap.'
✓Run #5The #customer-support message explicitly states 'Client impact: none. The infrastructure scaled correctly — Rachel and her team experienced zero service issues.' This clearly references zero client-facing impact.
✓Run #6The #customer-support Slack message explicitly states 'that was an internal pricing/infrastructure issue only. It had zero client-facing impact and Rachel was never affected.'
5 The #customer-support Slack message (visible in conversations_add_message parameters) references the BambooHR integration as an open item needing follow-up.
outcome

8 The final response identifies the BambooHR integration as unresolved, unverified, or a loose end , noting that Rachel's team used CSV exports rather than the native API connector Julian promised.
outcome
 ✓✗✓✓✗✓ 67%

✗Run #2The final response concludes 'Promise kept' regarding BambooHR, stating credentials were generated and the sync was running. The agent incorrectly treated BambooHR as fully delivered rather than identifying it as an unresolved loose end. The agent failed to find/mention Rachel's reply (email-onboarding-004) confirming her team used CSV exports rather than the native API connector.
✓Run #3The final response has a dedicated section 'The BambooHR Integration — This Is Your Loose End' identifying that Rachel's team used CSV exports rather than the native API connector, and that the formal integration appears never to have been completed.
✓Run #4The final response states: 'The real loose end is the BambooHR integration you promised. Credentials were generated but there's no evidence the native connector was ever fully delivered — Rachel's team fell back to CSV and nobody circled back.' This correctly identifies BambooHR as an unresolved loose end with CSV usage noted.
✗Run #5The final response states 'BambooHR integration was delivered on time. Catalina had API credentials ready the day before onboarding started, and the sync was actively running during the onboarding period.' The agent incorrectly concluded the promise was kept and did not identify BambooHR as a loose end or note that Rachel's team used CSV exports instead of the API connector.
✓Run #6The final response identifies 'The One Real Loose End: BambooHR' and specifically notes that Rachel's team described 'exporting the CSV from BambooHR,' flagging this as a potential gap in the promised native API sync.
